priorities.title: Outer hip and ankle stability — left side
priorities.detail: Drives left winging, left relevé wobble, and will directly compromise pointe work, pirouettes, and arabesque in the exam if unresolved

priorities.title: Shoulder girdle and épaulement
priorities.detail: Traps dominance is suppressing genuine upper back twist; affects Variation 2 performance mark and line across all technique sections

priorities.title: Weight placement — over the foot
priorities.detail: Posterior bias confirmed in parallel and in pointe work; upstream of pirouette axis, pointe work instability, and arabesque compensation

themes.name: Weight placement and balance
themes.summary: Posterior weight bias appearing across all contexts — barre, centre, pointe, allegro

themes.name: Outer hip and ankle stability
themes.summary: Left side weaker than right in rises and relevé; ankle winging; foot not firing fast enough in pointe work

themes.name: Shoulder girdle and épaulement
themes.summary: Traps dominance and shoulder blades riding up suppressing genuine épaulement and upper back twist

themes.name: Pirouette axis and preparation
themes.summary: Axis collapses before pirouette finishes — closing in 3rd; arm-driven rotation rather than hip-initiated

themes.name: Foot intrinsics and arch
themes.summary: Toe gripping and arch collapse under load in rises

themes.name: Arabesque mechanics
themes.summary: Second shoulder hiking; eyeline dropping; deep hip rotators releasing too early in fouetté

themes.name: Opposition and length under load
themes.summary: Passive on pointe entry; shoulders rising with arms; pirouettes closing short

themes.name: Pelvic alignment
themes.summary: Posterior pelvic tilt mistaken for neutral; improving as of May 2026 but proprioceptive sense still unreliable

exercises.name: Centre practice and pirouettes
corrections: Double pirouette en dehors: axis collapses before rotation completes — closing in 3rd not 5th
Double pirouette en dehors: arm-driven rotation rather than hip-initiated
Pirouette preparation: weight not fully forward in fondu — limited ankle bend restricting depth

exercises.name: Pirouette enchaînement
corrections: Same pirouette axis and preparation issues as AF-10 apply here
Lame duck (posés pirouettes en dehors): compact fourth under the body, not fifth

exercises.name: Adage
corrections: Arabesque: second shoulder hiking — outer hip and side body not stabilising torso
Fouetté en l'air: deep hip rotators releasing too early before arabesque extension
Arms need continuous movement through adage for fluidity

exercises.name: Variation 2
corrections: Poses: posterior weight bias, not getting over the box
Poses: retiré not getting up fast enough
Pas de bourrées: traveling rather than turning on the spot — retiré not at full height
Eyeline: up and over, not throwing the head back
Épaulement: more upper back twist needed
Outer ankle and foot not firing fast enough before weight transfer to pointe

itemsToRaise: Plié range gap: right 5cm past toes, left 10cm — gap still significant
Right ankle stiffer in turnout and through the point
Left ankle winging worse than right despite right being the injured side
Left single-leg relevé wobbles at top of range — ask whether left outer ankle weakness is confirmed or another cause
physioNotes.clearanceStatus: Right ankle grade 2 ligament tear — approx 9 weeks post-injury as of 23 May 2026. Returning to progressively increasing rehab and class participation. Fortnightly physio appointments.
restrictions: Cleared: Bilateral parallel pliés and rises at barre
Cleared: Single-leg static balance (monitor for soreness — flag if it comes on fast)
